Policy & Approvals

Approvals before spend is committed

Route bookings to the right approvers, apply structured policy logic, and maintain a full audit trail across every decision in your travel program.

Flexible policy control

Cinturon360 is being designed to support organisations with structured approval requirements. Policy logic should reflect the real complexity of how travel is governed — across different entities, teams, traveller types, destinations, and spend thresholds.

That means supporting approval paths that match the organisation, not just a simplified version of it.

Pre-trip approvals before spend is committed
Policy rules by entity, team, traveller type, or cost centre
Destination and supplier-based policy logic
Fare cap enforcement and class-of-travel rules
Out-of-policy handling with structured exceptions
Escalation paths for complex or high-value bookings
Full audit trail for every approval and policy outcome

Built for real programs

Travel programs do not all look the same. An enterprise organisation may need approval chains across multiple business units, different rules for different traveller categories, and exception handling for senior executives or unusual itineraries.

A government agency may need defensible decision records, stronger audit requirements, and tight policy controls around specific supplier or destination categories.

Cinturon360 is being built to support both, and the many program structures in between.

Exception handling

Out-of-policy bookings should not simply be blocked. The platform is being designed to support structured exception paths — where a traveller can request approval for an out-of-policy choice, a justification can be recorded, and the decision can be escalated or tracked.

Cinturon360 is coming soon

Cinturon360 is an upcoming travel governance and visibility platform designed to work with existing booking channels, apply policy and approvals, and deliver finance-grade reporting. Join the waitlist to follow the launch.

Join Waitlist Book a Demo
